zh.lifeSummary: 當William Gibbons於1504年出生在英國斯塔福爾瑟格利時，他的父親Sir Ralph Gibbons24歲，母親Katherine Lady of Bury Hatley Bedfordshire, Kilpeck14歲。他於1527年與Margaret Dudley在英國越可爾馬波萊頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1579年9月9日在英國斯塔福爾瑟格利享年75歲。

nameMeanings.name: Gibbons
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Gibbon , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Mayo): in Ireland, the name is frequent and has been Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. It is also found as a variant of Fitzgibbon .

variants.name: Gibbens
meanings: German: patronymic from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with geba ‘gift’.

variants.name: Gibbon
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Giboin (ancient Germanic Gebawin).
English: alternatively, a diminutive of the personal name   Gibb  .
Scottish and Irish: variant of   McGibbon  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When William Gibbons was born in 1504, in Sedgley, Staffordshire, England, his father, Sir Ralph Gibbons, was 24 and his mother, Katherine Lady of Bury Hatley Bedfordshire, Kilpeck, was 14. He married Margaret Dudley in 1527, in Mappleton, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 9 September 1579, in Sedgley, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 75.

Il a épousé Margaret Dudley en 1527, à Mappleton, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 9 septembre 1579, à Sedgley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William Gibbons(이)가 1504년 영국 스태퍼드실르 세지리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Ralph Gibbons(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Lady of Bury Hatley Bedfordshire, Kilpeck(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1527년 영국 요르크실르 마플턴에서 Margaret Dudley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1579년 9월 9일 영국 스태퍼드실르 세지리에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.
